records.ety: [F. parler speech, talk, fr. parler to speak, LL. parabolare, fr. L. parabola a comparison, parable, in LL., a word. See Parable, and cf. Parliament, Parlor.]

defs: Mutual discourse or conversation; discussion; hence, an oral conference with an enemy, as with regard to a truce.
quotes: We yield on parley, but are stormed in vain.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Parleyed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Parleying.]

defs: To speak with another; to confer on some point of mutual concern; to discuss orally; hence, specifically, to confer orally with an enemy; to treat with him by words, as on an exchange of prisoners, an armistice, or terms of peace.
quotes: They are at hand, To parley or to fight; therefore prepare.
